summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Tenant Portal feature. Backend API testing achieved 100% success rate (14/14 tests passed). Frontend testing confirmed all key features working correctly including registration, login, dashboard, document checklist, application status, appointments, messages, profile, and resources tabs.

fixed_during_testing.issue: MongoDB ObjectId serialization error causing 500 response
fixed_during_testing.fix: Added appointment.pop('_id', None) after insert_one to remove MongoDB _id

fixed_during_testing.issue: Similar ObjectId serialization issue in landlord message endpoint

passed_tests: Tenant registration creates account and returns token
Duplicate email registration rejected (400)
Tenant login with valid credentials returns token
Invalid credentials rejected (401)
Get tenant profile via /me endpoint
Update tenant profile with housing program info
Get document checklist (12 items for housing programs)
Get application status and stages
Submit application (change status to application_submitted)
Get appointments list
Create appointment (fixed ObjectId issue)
Get conversations list
Get housing resources (programs, tenant rights, FAQs)
Existing test tenant login (testtenant@test.com)
Landing page 'For Tenants' link navigation
Pro plan pricing shows $99/month or $82/month with Tenant Portal access
Tenant Portal Login page with proper form
Tenant Portal Register page with all fields
Dashboard with stats cards and application progress
Documents tab with checklist
Application Status tab with progress tracker
Appointments tab with Add Reminder button
Messages tab with conversations view
Profile tab with personal info and Edit Profile button
Resources tab with housing programs guide, tenant rights, FAQs
Logout functionality redirects to login
seed_data_creation: Test tenant accounts created during testing with prefix TEST_tenant_
context_for_next_testing_agent: Tenant Portal feature fully tested. All APIs working including auth, profile, document checklist, application status, appointments, messaging, and resources. The MongoDB ObjectId serialization issue was found and fixed in 4 insert_one locations. Frontend flows all working correctly with good UX/UI.
rca_of_issues: MongoDB insert_one() adds an _id field to the document dictionary which is an ObjectId. When FastAPI tries to serialize this as JSON response, it fails because ObjectId is not JSON serializable. Fix was to remove _id from the document before returning it.
